  • Library loading and sourcing

  • With reporting and more components

Module tests for RLum.OSL functions

Author

Dirk Mittelstraß

Published

March 10, 2026

This document collects scripts for developing, bug-fixing and pre-release validating the RLum.OSL workflow for evaluating the SAR protocol measurements as found on https://luminescence.de/OSL_tutorial.html

Found issues as for 2026-02-17:

  • …

ToDo:

  • …

Library loading and sourcing

Load the Luminescence package and its OSL SAR example data sets as well as OSLdecomposition

Show code 
library(Luminescence)
Welcome to the R package Luminescence version 1.1.2.9000-38 [Built: 2026-03-10 13:44:08 UTC]
Photon to electron: 'I am in charge!'
Show code 
library(OSLdecomposition)

Prepare data sets

Show code 
# Data provides with OSLdecomposition
data_path <- system.file("examples", 
                         "FB_10Gy_SAR.bin", 
                         package = "OSLdecomposition")
data_FB <- read_BIN2R(data_path, fastForward = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)


# Load example data from the Luminescence package
data(ExampleData.BINfileData, envir = environment())
data_LumRisoe <- Risoe.BINfileData2RLum.Analysis(CWOSL.SAR.Data)

# Load also XSYG data
data(ExampleData.XSYG)
data_LumLexsyg <- OSL.SARMeasurement #$Sequence.Object


data_sets <- list(data_LumRisoe, data_FB, data_LumLexsyg)
names(data_sets) <- c("FB calibration quartz", 
                      "BT607 (BIN.Example)",
                      "BT753 (XSYG.Example)")

Default workflow

Show code 
results <- list()
for (data_name in names(data_sets)) {
  
  cat("\n####", data_name, "#####\n\n")
  data_set <- data_sets[[data_name]]
  # Check the data for consistency and eventually manipulate the records for better results
  data_set <- RLum.OSL_correction(data_set)
  
  # Identify the OSL components globally (Step 1)
  data_set <- RLum.OSL_global_fitting(data_set)
  
  # Plot global curve
  plot_OSLcurve(data_set$FITTING$curve, 
                data_set$FITTING$components)
  
  # Separate OSL components in each record (Step 2)
  data_set <- RLum.OSL_decomposition(data_set) #, K = 3)
  
  results[[data_name]] <- data_set
  
}

#### FB calibration quartz #####

CORRECTION 1 ----- Check records for consistency in the detection settings -----
All OSL records have the same detection settings.

CORRECTION 2 ----- Remove not stimulated measurement parts -----
No measurement parts with stimulation light turned off detected

CORRECTION 3 ----- Normalize x-axis -----
First x-axis value is already equal channel width value for all records.

CORRECTION 4 ----- Limit measurement duration -----
Reduced length of 336 OSL records from 40 s to 20 s

CORRECTION 5 ----- Correct for PMT pulse-pair resolution -----
Every channel with a signal higher than  5270  counts/sec will be increased. 
312  of  336   OSL  records contained signal values which needed to be corrected.
Signal statistics or corrected first channels:
Median increase =  0.11  % / Maximum increase =  1.14  %

STEP 1.1 ----- Build global average curve from all CW-OSL curves -----
384 records were not suitable.
Built arithmetic mean curve from 336 OSL records

STEP 1.2 ----- Perform multi-exponential curve fitting -----
Decay rates (s^-1):
Cycle       Comp. 1     Comp. 2     Comp. 3         RSS     F-value
K = 1         3.121                           1.359e+06         Inf 
K = 2         3.463     0.05759               5.883e+04        5480 
K = 3         4.022       1.264     0.02948        3030        4549 
Left loop because maximum number of allowed components K is reached
-> The F-test suggests the K = 3 model 
STEP 2.1 ----- Define signal bin intervals -----
Find intervals with lowest component cross correlation by maximising the denominator determinant in Cramers rule:
Maximum determinant = 0.1377 with interval dividing channels at i = 10, 73 

STEP 2.2 ----- Decompose each  OSL  curve -----
Calculate signal intensity n in each OSL  by ' det+nls ' algorithm with empiric error estimation

Table of input decay constants and signal bin intervals:
             lambda t.start t.end ch.start ch.end
Component 1 4.02213    0.00  0.40        1     10
Component 2 1.26356    0.40  2.92       11     73
Component 3 0.02948    2.92 20.00       74    500

Process aliquots: oooooooooooooooooooooooo
-> Successfully decomposed 336 records

Initial signal statistics:
            Minimum 25% quantile    Median 75% quantile Maximum
Component 1  -0.215        0.796     0.842        0.886   0.963
Component 2  -0.341        0.093     0.124        0.158   0.704
Component 3   0.004        0.012     0.027        0.037   0.951
-> Dominating signal component at measurement start: "Component 1"
 (If not knowing the correct component, take this one)


#### BT607 (BIN.Example) #####

CORRECTION 1 ----- Check records for consistency in the detection settings -----
All OSL records have the same detection settings.

CORRECTION 2 ----- Remove not stimulated measurement parts -----
No measurement parts with stimulation light turned off detected

CORRECTION 3 ----- Normalize x-axis -----
First x-axis value is already equal channel width value for all records.

CORRECTION 4 ----- Limit measurement duration -----
Reduced length of 154 OSL records from 99.8 s to 20.07988 s

CORRECTION 5 ----- Correct for PMT pulse-pair resolution -----
Every channel with a signal higher than  5270  counts/sec will be increased. 
130  of  154   OSL  records contained signal values which needed to be corrected.
Signal statistics or corrected first channels:
Median increase =  0.13  % / Maximum increase =  0.82  %

STEP 1.1 ----- Build global average curve from all CW-OSL curves -----
Built arithmetic mean curve from 154 OSL records

STEP 1.2 ----- Perform multi-exponential curve fitting -----
Decay rates (s^-1):
Cycle       Comp. 1     Comp. 2     Comp. 3         RSS     F-value
K = 1        0.7569                            8.51e+05         Inf 
K = 2        0.7907     0.03437               9.908e+04       747.5 
K = 3         10.08      0.8129     0.05326        5819        1563 
Left loop because maximum number of allowed components K is reached
-> The F-test suggests the K = 3 model 
STEP 2.1 ----- Define signal bin intervals -----
Find intervals with lowest component cross correlation by maximising the denominator determinant in Cramers rule:
Maximum determinant = 0.3226 with interval dividing channels at i = 3, 33 

STEP 2.2 ----- Decompose each  OSL  curve -----
Calculate signal intensity n in each OSL  by ' det+nls ' algorithm with empiric error estimation

Table of input decay constants and signal bin intervals:
              lambda   t.start      t.end ch.start ch.end
Component 1 10.07842 0.0000000  0.2996997        1      3
Component 2  0.81288 0.2996997  3.2966968        4     33
Component 3  0.05326 3.2966968 20.0798805       34    201

Process aliquots: ooooooooooo
-> Successfully decomposed 154 records

Initial signal statistics:
            Minimum 25% quantile    Median 75% quantile Maximum
Component 1  -0.295       -0.101    -0.036       -0.002   0.523
Component 2  -0.786        0.983     1.017        1.073   1.147
Component 3   0.004        0.015     0.021        0.031   1.328
-> Dominating signal component at measurement start: "Component 2"
 (If not knowing the correct component, take this one)


#### BT753 (XSYG.Example) #####

CORRECTION 1 ----- Check records for consistency in the detection settings -----
All OSL records have the same detection settings.

CORRECTION 2 ----- Remove not stimulated measurement parts -----
No measurement parts with stimulation light turned off detected

CORRECTION 3 ----- Normalize x-axis -----
First x-axis value is already equal channel width value for all records.

CORRECTION 4 ----- Limit measurement duration -----
Reduced length of 14 OSL records from 50 s to 20 s

CORRECTION 5 ----- Correct for PMT pulse-pair resolution -----
Every channel with a signal higher than  5270  counts/sec will be increased. 
5  of  14   OSL  records contained signal values which needed to be corrected.
Signal statistics or corrected first channels:
Median increase =  0.02  % / Maximum increase =  0.06  %

STEP 1.1 ----- Build global average curve from all CW-OSL curves -----
110 records were not suitable.
Built arithmetic mean curve from 14 OSL records

STEP 1.2 ----- Perform multi-exponential curve fitting -----
Decay rates (s^-1):
Cycle       Comp. 1     Comp. 2     Comp. 3         RSS     F-value
K = 1         1.848                           4.622e+04         Inf 
K = 2         2.034     0.02403                    1708        2554 
K = 3         2.181       0.614   0.0003104       992.7        69.9 
Left loop because F-test value (F = 69.9) fell below threshold value (F = 150)
-> The F-test suggests the K = 2 model 
STEP 2.1 ----- Define signal bin intervals -----
Find intervals with lowest component cross correlation by maximising the denominator determinant in Cramers rule:
Maximum determinant = 0.3295 with interval dividing channels at i = 17 

STEP 2.2 ----- Decompose each  OSL  curve -----
Calculate signal intensity n in each OSL  by ' det+nls ' algorithm with empiric error estimation

Table of input decay constants and signal bin intervals:
             lambda t.start t.end ch.start ch.end
Component 1 2.03356     0.0   1.7        1     17
Component 2 0.02403     1.7  20.0       18    200

Process aliquots: o
-> Successfully decomposed 14 records

Initial signal statistics:
            Minimum 25% quantile    Median 75% quantile Maximum
Component 1   0.021        0.920     0.947        0.981   0.992
Component 2   0.008        0.019     0.053        0.080   0.979
-> Dominating signal component at measurement start: "Component 1"
 (If not knowing the correct component, take this one)

Analysing De values

Show code 
for (data_set in results) {
  
  De_vals <- 
    analyse_SAR.CWOSL(data_set,
                      OSL.component =
                        data_set$DECOMPOSITION$dominating.component,
                      plot = TRUE, plot_onePage = TRUE, verbose = FALSE)
  
  plot_AbanicoPlot(De_vals)
}
Warning: [fit_DoseResponseCurve()] 1 values with 0 for Lx/Tx detected, replaced
by 2.22044604925031e-16
Warning: [plot_AbanicoPlot()] Data set 1 empty or consisting of only 1 row,
removed
[plot_AbanicoPlot()] Error: After removing invalid entries, nothing is plotted

With reporting and more components

Show code 
for (data_name in names(data_sets)) {
  
  cat("\n####", data_name, "#####\n\n")
  data_set <- data_sets[[data_name]]
  
  # Check the data for consistency and eventually manipulate the records for better results
  data_set <- RLum.OSL_correction(data_set)
  
  # Identify the OSL components globally (Step 1)
  data_set <- RLum.OSL_global_fitting(data_set,
                                      K_maximum = 4,
                                      stimulation_intensity = 80,
                                      report = TRUE)
  
  # Separate OSL components in each record (Step 2)
  data_set <- RLum.OSL_decomposition(data_set, report = TRUE)
  
}

#### FB calibration quartz #####

CORRECTION 1 ----- Check records for consistency in the detection settings -----
All OSL records have the same detection settings.

CORRECTION 2 ----- Remove not stimulated measurement parts -----
No measurement parts with stimulation light turned off detected

CORRECTION 3 ----- Normalize x-axis -----
First x-axis value is already equal channel width value for all records.

CORRECTION 4 ----- Limit measurement duration -----
Reduced length of 336 OSL records from 40 s to 20 s

CORRECTION 5 ----- Correct for PMT pulse-pair resolution -----
Every channel with a signal higher than  5270  counts/sec will be increased. 
312  of  336   OSL  records contained signal values which needed to be corrected.
Signal statistics or corrected first channels:
Median increase =  0.11  % / Maximum increase =  1.14  %

STEP 1.1 ----- Build global average curve from all CW-OSL curves -----
384 records were not suitable.
Built arithmetic mean curve from 336 OSL records

STEP 1.2 ----- Perform multi-exponential curve fitting -----
Decay rates (s^-1):
Cycle       Comp. 1     Comp. 2     Comp. 3     Comp. 4         RSS     F-value
K = 1         3.121                                       1.359e+06         Inf 
K = 2         3.463     0.05759                           5.883e+04        5480 
K = 3         4.022       1.264     0.02948                    3030        4549 
K = 4         4.946       2.677      0.4348     0.01584       329.1        2019 
Left loop because maximum number of allowed components K is reached
-> The F-test suggests the K = 4 model 

Photoionisation cross sections (cm^2):
Cycle     Comp. 1              Comp. 2              Comp. 3              Comp. 4             
K = 1    1.65e-17             
K = 2    1.83e-17             3.04e-19 (Slow2)     
K = 3    2.12e-17 (Fast)      6.68e-18 (Medium)    1.56e-19 (Slow2)     
K = 4    2.61e-17 (Fast)      1.41e-17             2.3e-18              8.37e-20             
-> Most known quartz OSL components found in the K = 4 model 

STEP 1.3 ----- Create report -----
This process can take a few minutes...
(time needed: 21.69 s)

Open HTML report in the systems standard browser
STEP 2.1 ----- Define signal bin intervals -----
Find intervals with lowest component cross correlation by maximising the denominator determinant in Cramers rule:
Maximum determinant = 0.01597 with interval dividing channels at i = 6, 29, 149 

STEP 2.2 ----- Decompose each  OSL  curve -----
Calculate signal intensity n in each OSL  by ' det+nls ' algorithm with empiric error estimation

Table of input decay constants and signal bin intervals:
             lambda t.start t.end ch.start ch.end
Fast        4.94583    0.00  0.24        1      6
Component 2 2.67731    0.24  1.16        7     29
Component 3 0.43475    1.16  5.96       30    149
Component 4 0.01584    5.96 20.00      150    500

Process aliquots: oooooooooooooooooooooooo
-> Successfully decomposed 336 records

Initial signal statistics:
            Minimum 25% quantile    Median 75% quantile Maximum
Fast         -0.955        0.392     0.459        0.520   0.681
Component 2  -0.141        0.427     0.477        0.536   1.127
Component 3  -0.152        0.015     0.025        0.036   0.422
Component 4   0.003        0.010     0.022        0.030   0.997
-> Dominating signal component at measurement start: "Component 2"
 (If not knowing the correct component, take this one)

STEP 2.3 ----- Create report -----
This process can take a few minutes...
(time needed: 52.18 s)

Open HTML report in the systems standard browser

#### BT753 (XSYG.Example) #####

CORRECTION 1 ----- Check records for consistency in the detection settings -----
All OSL records have the same detection settings.

CORRECTION 2 ----- Remove not stimulated measurement parts -----
No measurement parts with stimulation light turned off detected

CORRECTION 3 ----- Normalize x-axis -----

Every channel with a signal higher than  5270  counts/sec will be increased.
